heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks on the Great Ocean Road
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip along the Great Ocean Road? We compared the best family holiday and caravan parks on the Great Ocean Road so you can quickly find the right park for your kids, budget and travel style.
This is Australia's best family road trip, and the parks along it are genuinely strong: a 92-scored beachfront resort at Apollo Bay, an indoor waterslide complex at Anglesea and koalas wandering through camp at Wye River.
local_knowledge: The smart family move is basing yourself in one or two spots rather than moving every night. Apollo Bay sits mid-route and BIG4 Pisces is the standout park on the entire road, with nightly light shows, a pump track and a heated pool across from the beach. Anglesea suits the Melbourne end, and BIG4 Anglesea's indoor heated pool with waterslides is the region's wet-weather trump card. Kennett River has the most reliable wild koala spotting on the road; look up on the walk behind the caravan park. The Twelve Apostles are 90 minutes past Apollo Bay, so do them as a day trip and time it for late afternoon light. The Otway treetop walk and Erskine Falls at Lorne break up the drive nicely.

if_we_were_booking.label: The full resort experience
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose BIG4 Apollo Bay Pisces, the highest-scored park on the road, for the heated pool, pump track, nightly light shows and beachfront position mid-route.

if_we_were_booking.label: Wet-weather insurance
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose BIG4 Anglesea for the big indoor heated pool with waterslides, splash park and toddler rooms. In Victorian coastal weather, that indoor pool earns its keep.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Stay two or three nights in one base rather than hopping nightly. Apollo Bay puts the Twelve Apostles, the Otways and Kennett River's koalas all within day-trip range, and the kids get to actually enjoy the park.

faq.question: Is the Great Ocean Road good for kids?
faq.answer: Yes, if you pace it. Base yourself at one or two parks, keep drive days under two hours and mix beach time with short walks. The winding road can make backseat passengers queasy if you rush it.

faq.question: What is the best family holiday park on the Great Ocean Road?
faq.answer: BIG4 Apollo Bay Pisces scores 92/100, the highest on the road, with a heated pool, water play area, pump track, jumping pillow and nightly light shows across from the beach.

faq.question: Where can kids see wild koalas?
faq.answer: Kennett River, between Lorne and Apollo Bay, is the most reliable spot. Walk up Grey River Road behind the caravan park and look up. Early morning and late afternoon are best.

faq.question: How far are the Twelve Apostles from Apollo Bay?
faq.answer: About 90 minutes drive. Go late afternoon for the best light and fewer coaches, and add Loch Ard Gorge next door, kids usually prefer it.

faq.question: Which park is best in bad weather?
faq.answer: BIG4 Anglesea. The large indoor heated pool with waterslides, splash park and toddler areas keeps a family busy through a full day of rain.

faq.question: Are the beaches safe for young children?
faq.answer: Swim between the flags at patrolled beaches like Lorne, Apollo Bay and Anglesea in season. The ocean beaches carry strong rips, so keep young kids to the patrolled stretches.

faq.question: How many days do you need?
faq.answer: Three to five days works well for families. One or two nights at the Anglesea or Lorne end, then two or three at Apollo Bay covers the road without rushing.

faq.question: Is the drive hard with a caravan?
faq.answer: It's winding but sealed and well-maintained. Take it slow between Lorne and Apollo Bay, use the pull-over bays, and avoid towing it as a day-trip road, park the van and drive light.

faq.question: What are the best free family activities?
faq.answer: Koala spotting at Kennett River, Erskine Falls at Lorne, the beaches, and the lookouts the whole way along cost nothing.

faq.question: How far in advance should you book?
faq.answer: Apollo Bay and Anglesea book out well ahead for summer school holidays. Shoulder season, March to May and September to November, is quieter and the road is at its best.

hero_tagline: Finding the perfect holiday park along the Great Ocean Road means discovering that special place where your kids can explore nature while you relax, creating those precious family memories you'll treasure forever.
